title: 10 best herbal creams for over bleeding during periods

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its unique composition. Witch hazel contains tannins, a type of polyphenol that has natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it helps to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding and promoting clotting. Additionally, witch hazel's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote healing. As a result, American witch hazel creams provide effective relief from heavy menstrual bleeding, allowing women to manage their symptoms with confidence and comfort.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in Witch Hazel help to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and thus alleviating excessive menstrual bleeding.
Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of Witch Hazel help to dry up excess moisture and reduce swelling in the vaginal area, which can contribute to heavy bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Witch Hazel contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps associated with menstruation, which can exacerbate excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce blood flow and alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
Barks: The barks of the American Witch Hazel contain tannins, which constrict blood vessels and help to stop excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry hamamelis virginiana leaves in a warm dark place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Combine 100 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves with 500 milliliters of cold pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler until melted and well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease menstrual cramps, thereby reducing the amount of blood lost during menstruation. Additionally, pot marigold's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties may help to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate heavy bleeding. By addressing these underlying issues, pot marigold creams can provide relief from excessive bleeding and promote a more comfortable and balanced menstrual experience.
remedy_properties: Astringent and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold cream's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding and inflammation associated with heavy menstrual flow.

Antioxidant and Free Radical Scavenging Properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which may exacerbate heavy bleeding during periods, helping to protect the uterine lining from damage.

Emollient and Soothing Properties: Pot marigold cream's emollient and soothing properties help to calm and comfort irritated skin and mucous membranes, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy healing of the vagina and vulva.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce excessive bleeding by constricting blood vessels.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and mucilages that soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers 2 tablespoons and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground calendula powder with 8 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it for 3 hours.
Allow the mixture to cool and then add 4 ounces of beeswax pellets and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and refrigerate it at 50-60°f for 24 hours.
Once set, remove the creams from the refrigerator and allow them to come to room temperature before labeling and storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and ease blood flow. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict the blood vessels, thereby reducing excessive bleeding and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, stinging nettle's astringent properties can also help to absorb excess blood, further minimizing menstrual flow. As a result, stinging nettle creams can provide relief from heavy bleeding, cramping, and other symptoms associated with an irregular or heavy period.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Stinging nettle creams contain astringent compounds that help constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and minimizing bleeding.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Vasoprotective properties: Nettle's vasoprotective compounds help strengthen and stabilize blood vessel walls, preventing them from rupturing or leaking excessively during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, nettle roots help to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps associated with heavy bleeding.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, nettle seeds promote healthy uterine function and reduce bleeding by regulating prostaglandins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh nettle leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Steep the chopped nettle leaves in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Add 30 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil to the infusion and heat it gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into a sterile jar and allow it to cool and thicken for at least two hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a combination of natural herbs that work together to strengthen and tone the uterine lining, reducing excessive blood flow. The calming and anti-inflammatory properties of the herbs soothe the uterus, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding. Additionally, the antispasmodic effects help to regulate blood vessel contractions, further alleviating symptoms of over bleeding. This natural remedy promotes a healthy menstrual cycle and provides relief from the discomfort and inconvenience of excessive bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Astringent properties: The astringent properties of the cream help to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the affected area, thereby reducing heavy bleeding.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the cream help to protect the uterine lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent excessive bleeding.
Barks: The barks of the broad-leaved lime plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to stop bleeding and promote clotting.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried flowers of tilia platyphyllos in amounts of 100 grams.
Combine the dried flowers with carrier oils such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at a temperature range of 60 to 70°c for two hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass jars, discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to airtight containers and storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to constrict blood vessels and reduce menstrual flow. The herbal extract present in highbush cranberry creams contains proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to help regulate excessive bleeding by reducing inflammation and improving uterine tone. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties soothe and calm the vaginal area, further reducing discomfort associated with heavy bleeding during menstruation. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, women can experience relief from over bleeding and a more manageable period.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Highbush cranberry creams contain tannins, which have astringent effects on the mucous membranes, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in highbush cranberry creams help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps associated with menstruation, thereby alleviating excessive bleeding.

Stabilizing estrogen levels: Highbush cranberry creams contain isoflavones, which have been shown to stabilize estrogen levels, regulate menstrual cycles, and alleviate symptoms of heavy bleeding, including flooding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have astringent properties, which help to stop excessive bleeding by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks contain tannins that can help to shrink the size of blood vessels, reducing bleeding and promoting clotting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Combine the powder with 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 10% glycerin in a glass jar for 24 hours to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of gentle pressure.
Heat 100g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes, then slowly pour the infused oil mixture into it while whisking until fully incorporated.
Pour the warm cream mixture into sterilized glass containers and seal them immediately; store at room temperature for up to six months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its unique composition that targets the root cause of excessive menstrual flow. The extract from the maidenhair fern, specifically the Pteridium aquilinum, has been traditionally used to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by constricting blood vessels and regulating blood platelet function. This natural approach soothes the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and promoting a more balanced hormonal environment, ultimately leading to a decrease in excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effect: The cream from Maidenhair Tree (Ginkgo biloba) has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to dilate blood vessels, reducing menstrual bleeding by increasing blood flow and promoting platelet aggregation. This property reduces heavy bleeding during periods.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: The cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause contractions and lead to heavier bleeding. This combined effect helps minimize excessive bleeding.

Hormone-regulating effects: Maidenhair Tree cream contains flavonoids and terpenes that have a regulatory effect on estrogen levels, helping to balance hormones and reduce the severity of menstrual symptoms like heavy bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood clotting, making them effective in treating excessive menstrual bleeding.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders due to their ability to regulate uterine contractions and reduce heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 20 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture to dissolve the beeswax completely and then remove from heat, stirring occasionally until it reaches a consistency similar to honey for 5 minutes.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of tea tree oil to the cooled mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized glass jar and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, allowing it to cool completely before use within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain bioflavonoids, which are natural antihemorrhagic agents that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding. The creams also contain astringent properties that help to shrink the diameter of blood vessels, further reducing blood flow and alleviating heavy bleeding. Additionally, bitter orange creams have been shown to have a soothing effect on the uterine lining, helping to calm and normalize menstrual flow, providing relief from discomfort and anxiety caused by prolonged bleeding.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Helps to strengthen blood vessels and improve overall circulation, reducing the likelihood of excessive bleeding.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles that may be contributing to heavy bleeding.
Citrus bioflavonoids: Have a vasoconstrictive effect, which can help to narrow blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the uterus, thereby reducing bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
Rhyzomes: Contain compounds that have astringent properties, which can help to absorb and stop heavy menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry citrus x aurantium peel in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks or until brittle.
Grind the dried citrus x aurantium peel into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10% of the powdered citrus x aurantium peel with 90% sweet almond oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled cream mixture until light and fluffy using an electric mixer or whisk, then store in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry rosa x rugosa flowers by gathering fresh petals in the morning and drying them for at least 7 days in a cool dark place.
Combine dried flowers with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the wax melts and infuses into the oil for 1 hour.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for further preparation for 20 minutes.
Mix the reserved liquid with coconut oil and vitamin e oil, blending well to combine and maintaining a temperature below 160°f during processing for 10 minutes.
Pour the blended mixture into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature before storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its ability to balance hormones and reduce inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in the summer months ensuring proper identification and correct dosages.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air-drying for 3 weeks to preserve potency.
Combine dried hypericum perforatum with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler slowly melting the mixture under gentle heat for 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and calendula extract to the melted mixture stirring well and continuing to simmer for an additional 15 minutes to combine ingredients fully.
Pour the cooled and thickened cream into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months with a shelf life of 2 weeks once opened.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems and measure out 30 grams of the herb mixture.
Combine the herb mixture with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or olive oil in a heatproof glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until the oil reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius stirring occasionally.

Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars, label them with the date and ingredients, and store in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f.

Buds: Rosemary buds contain flavonoids, which help to reduce blood vessel constriction, thereby alleviating heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them in shade for two weeks.
Grind the dried herbs into fine powder using mortar and pestle, then sift through a mesh strainer.
Mix 20 grams of the herb powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it gently for one hour.
Remove from heat and let it infuse for four hours before filtering the mixture through cheesecloth or cotton cloth into a clean glass jar.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the filtered liquid, melting them in a double boiler while stirring constantly for five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and constriction that can lead to excessive bleeding. Additionally, thyme has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, promoting clotting and slowing down menstrual flow. By applying thyme cream topically, women can experience relief from heavy or prolonged bleeding, allowing for a more comfortable and manageable period.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Thyme contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
Essential Oils: Thyme essential oils, such as thymol, have antispasmodic properties that can help to ease uterine cramps and reduce heavy bleeding by relaxing the uterine muscles.

Antioxidants: Thyme creams often contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, leading to a decrease in excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
Buds: Thyme buds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a sunny morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Mix 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris with 40 grams of sweet almond oil and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and stir until melted, then remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer for 5 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for over bleeding during periods are topical ointments infused with natural ingredients that help alleviate excessive menstrual bleeding. These creams work by reducing inflammation, soothing cramps, and promoting blood clotting, thereby decreasing flow. Examples of herbal creams that help include those containing witch hazel, aloe vera, and chamomile, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. By using these creams, women can experience improved menstrual hygiene, reduced anxiety, and increased confidence, ultimately enhancing their overall quality of life during this time.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat over bleeding during periods with herbal creams is to apply a combination of soothing and astringent herbs topically. Aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort, while witch hazel and yarrow contain tannins that constrict blood vessels and promote clotting. Applying these herbs in the form of creams or ointments can effectively alleviate excessive bleeding and restore menstrual balance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal creams are hemorrhoids, varicose veins, and eczema. These conditions can cause discomfort, itching, and inflammation, making them ideal candidates for natural treatments. Herbal creams containing ingredients like witch hazel, aloe vera, and tea tree oil can soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing pain and discomfort while promoting healing.
